item.title: Iran to let nuclear inspections resume as talks continue, Vance says
item.description: Vance says Iran agreed to let international nuclear inspectors back into the country during a "very, very good" first day of negotiations with the U.S.

item.title: Polymarket launches probe after report alleges deceptive marketing
item.description: A Wall Street Journal investigation found that the prediction market paid content creators to produce videos of fake trades purporting to show big financial gains.

item.title: Clive Davis, legendary music executive, dies at 94
item.description: Legendary music executive Clive Davis, who helped shape the careers of generations of artists including Janis Joplin, Bruce Springsteen and Whitney Houston, has died at 94. Jim Aswad, executive editor for Variety, joins CBS News to discuss Davis' life.

item.title: Supreme Court reinstates murder conviction in disappearance of Etan Patz
item.description: Etan Patz walked out of his New York City home headed for a school bus stop in May of 1979. He never made it to school and has never been found.

item.title: How much house can you afford with a $75,000 salary right now?
item.description: Thinking about buying a home? Here's what a $75,000 salary can realistically support in today's market.

item.title: LAUSD Superintendent Alberto Carvalho resigns amid FBI investigation
item.description: Nearly four months after federal agents raided his home and district headquarters, Superintendent Alberto Carvalho announced his resignation as the head of the Los Angeles Unified School District on Sunday.

item.title: Judge in Charlie Kirk case to make ruling over prosecutors' media comments
item.description: A Utah judge in the Charlie Kirk case is set to rule on potential sanctions against prosecutors over comments allegedly made to the media. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has more.

item.title: Nearly one in every four people who contract Ebola die, data shows
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in Uganda and the Congo is worsening as health officials continue to battle minimal staffing and resources, as well as misinformation. CBS News' Ramy Inocencio has more.

item.title: Woman recalls finding mom "under the rubble" after Tesla crashed into home
item.description: Officials said a Tesla, which the driver said was on autopilot, crashed into a home on Friday in Katy, Texas, killing one person.

item.title: New York, South Carolina set to hold key primaries
item.description: Voters are set to head to the polls Tuesday in New York, South Carolina, Utah and Maryland for key primary races ahead of the 2026 midterm elections. CBS News' Fin Gomez has more.

item.title: U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 2, leaves 6 survivors, in the Caribbean
item.description: The U.S. military has conducted another strike against a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the Caribbean, killing two and leaving six survivors, the U.S. Southern Command said.

item.title: Judge quashes 6 grand jury subpoenas against Minnesota officials
item.description: A U.S. District Judge ruled the Trump administration's use of grand jury subpoenas against Minnesota state and local officials was retaliatory and unlawful, finding no legitimate investigatory justification for them.

item.title: What are today's mortgage and mortgage refinance interest rates?
item.description: Knowing today's mortgage rates can help new homebuyers and homeowners looking to lock in a good deal.

item.title: What happens if you don't pay a deceased person's credit card debt?
item.description: A loved one's unpaid card balances don't always just disappear after death. Here's what their families should know.

item.title: Trump expected to meet with defense contractors Wednesday amid Iran peace talks
item.description: The Trump administration has been ratcheting up pressure on defense contractors to prioritize production and American manufacturing capabilities over shareholder payouts.

item.title: "Alligator Alcatraz" shutting down permanently, sources tell CBS News Miami
item.description: Closing Florida's "Alligator Alcatraz" has been the subject of speculation for the past two months.

item.title: Interstellar comet may be oldest object ever seen in solar system
item.description: Exactly where the comet 3I/ATLAS came from within the Milky Way remains a mystery.

item.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the man likely to be Britain's next prime minister?
item.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer is resigning, and the man widely expected to replace him is a fellow Labour Party lawmaker known as the "King of the North."

item.title: Clive Davis, legendary music executive, dies at age 94
item.description: Clive Davis helped shape the careers of music stars including including Janis Joplin, Bruce Springsteen and Whitney Houston.

item.title: Protests sparked by Kushner-backed resort plans gain steam in Albania
item.description: Plans backed by investors including Jared Kushner for a luxury resort in Albania drew protests that have grown into an anti-corruption movement.

item.title: Trump plan would increase citizenship application fee by $570
item.description: A Trump administration plan would charge legal immigrants seeking citizenship $570 more in application fees while eliminating waivers and fee reductions for low-income applicants.

item.title: Endangered pregnant whale found dead on bow of cruise ship in Alaska
item.description: A pregnant fin whale was found dead on the bow of a cruise ship in Alaska last week. Fin whales are endangered and particularly threatened by vessel strikes.

item.title: These are the issues that could derail a U.S. and Iran deal
item.description: While Vice President JD Vance expressed optimism after wrapping up two days of talks with Iran, questions remain about certain sticking points that could derail negotiations. CBS News' Aaron Navarro and Courtney Kealy have more.

item.title: Alan Greenspan, Fed chair under 4 U.S. presidents, dies at age 100
item.description: Alan Greenspan's lengthy reign at the Federal Reserve coincided with a period of stability from the mid-1980s until 2007.

item.title: A 4-year-old needed a liver transplant. A stranger stepped in to help.
item.description: Anh Nguyen, 52, gave two people the gift of life through organ donation – including a young girl suffering from a rare genetic condition.

item.title: Vance says Iran will allow IAEA nuclear inspectors as U.S. deal talks continue
item.description: Vice President JD Vance claimed at a press conference on Monday morning that the U.S. and Iran are making notable progress in their talks. Vance said Tehran will also allow IAEA inspectors to enter the country to survey nuclear facilities. CBS News' Aaron Navarro and Courtney Kealy report.

item.title: Trump alleges vandals are responsible for ongoing problems at Reflecting Pool
item.description: President Trump is alleging, without proof, that vandals are to blame for recent issues at the Reflecting Pool in D.C. and says several people have been arrested. The Washington landmark has been plagued with algae and now there is paint peeling. Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: Where negotiations stand between U.S. and Iran as Vance leads delegation in Switzerland
item.description: Vice President JD Vance is in Switzerland as part of a 60-day effort to work out a peace plan with Iran. But as he negotiates, President Trump has issued new threats to Iran if a deal doesn't work out. Weijia Jiang reports.

item.title: Billionaire Rick Jackson beats Trump-endorsed Burt Jones in Georgia Senate runoff race
item.description: Lt. Gov. Burt Jones entered the Republican runoff for Georgia governor as the frontrunner, backed by President Trump, but billionaire healthcare executive Rick Jackson has won the runoff, according to a CBS News projection. Jackson will face Democrat Keisha Lance Bottoms in the November general election. CBS News political director Fin Gómez reports.

item.title: Search all the contracts, grants and leases DOGE says it has cut
item.description: CBS News is publishing searchable databases of all of the contracts, grants and leases DOGE says it has canceled.
